questions.text: What is the empirical relationship between mean, median and mode?
questions.subject: Mathematics Reasoning

options.explanation: Mode = 3 Median − 2 Mean – Correct. Standard empirical formula
Mean = Mode + Median – Incorrect. Not a standard relation
Median = Mean + Mode – Incorrect. Doesn’t apply in empirical stats
Mean = 3 Mode − 2 Median – Incorrect. Inverted and invalid formula

questions.explanation: Mode = 3 Median − 2 Mean – Correct. Standard empirical formula

questions.text: Which measure is NOT affected by extreme values?
questions.subject: Mathematics Reasoning

options.explanation: Mean – Incorrect. Mean is highly affected by outliers
Median – Correct. Median only depends on the middle value(s)
Mode – Incorrect. Mode may change but not as sensitive as mean
Range – Incorrect. Range is directly affected by extreme values

questions.explanation: Median – Correct. Median only depends on the middle value(s)

questions.text: In a symmetric distribution, the correct relationship among mean, median, and mode is:
questions.subject: Mathematics Reasoning

options.explanation: Mean = Median = Mode – Correct. Symmetry ensures all central measures are equal
Mean > Median > Mode – Incorrect. Happens in right-skewed distributions
Mode > Median > Mean – Incorrect. Happens in left-skewed distributions
Mean ≠ Median = Mode – Incorrect. Doesn’t describe symmetry properly

questions.explanation: Mean = Median = Mode – Correct. Symmetry ensures all central measures are equal
